data.text: This bead crosses the size threshold — 10 ACs spanning a new `cli/internal/checks/` package, integration into the 1693-line `execute_bead_loop.go`, a new bead schema field with event recording, a new `ddx ac` CLI command, plus two e2e tests. It's coherent in theme but mixes design + implementation + integration + e2e in unrelated subsystems. Decomposing into three reviewable slices.

data.output:
{
  "acceptance": "1. Protocol spec: .ddx/checks/*.yaml schema documented; Go types in cli/internal/checks/protocol.go match the spec; JSON output schema documented.
2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.
4. ddx try integration: pre_merge hook runs ALL applicable checks (filtered by applies_to) in parallel before merge-back; any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows the check fixture wrote its result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.",
  "claimed-at": "2026-05-02T20:49:32Z",
  "claimed-machine": "eitri",
  "claimed-pid": "3924346",
  "created_at": "2026-05-02T20:34:44.62487483Z",
  "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",
  "execute-loop-heartbeat-at": "2026-05-02T20:50:02.271788575Z",
  "id": "ddx-a946c744",
  "issue_type": "task",
  "labels": [
    "phase:2",
    "area:agent",
    "area:checks",
    "kind:platform",
    "prevention"
  ],
  "metrics": {
    "attempt_count": 0,
    "total_tokens": 0,
    "total_cost_usd": 0,
    "avg_duration_ms": 0
  },
  "owner": "ddx",
  "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
  "priority": 0,
  "status": "in_progress",
  "title": "ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-02T20:50:02.271789784Z"
}

input.command:
ddx bead create --title "checks protocol package: types + loader + runner + ddx ac CLI" --priority 0 --labels "phase:2,area:checks,kind:platform" --description "Slice 1 of parent ddx-a946c744. Build the language-agnostic check protocol package and standalone CLI. NO integration with execute-bead loop yet (separate child bead).

CONTEXT
See parent ddx-a946c744 for full design. This slice creates the foundational package and the developer-facing CLI for running checks against the current worktree.

SCOPE
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema (Check, AppliesTo) + JSON I/O types (Result, Violation); status enum (pass/block/error); CheckResult JSON schema doc as Go comment
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml; reject invalid YAML and unknown fields with file:line; return []Check
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — given []Check + invocation context (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID), run all applicable checks in parallel via os/exec; read \${EVIDENCE_DIR}/\${name}.json; non-zero exit → status=error; return []Result
- cli/internal/checks/applies_test.go etc — table-driven tests for path glob matching and label matching against AppliesTo
- cli/internal/checks/loader_test.go — valid yaml round-trips; invalid yaml fails loudly with location
- cli/internal/checks/runner_test.go — fakecheck shell-script fixture under testdata/ that writes pass / block / error results; verifies env injection, parallel exec, exit-code handling, missing result file → error
- cli/cmd/ac.go — \`ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>]\` subcommand wired to runner; reads bead via existing bead store; computes DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD from bead metadata + current HEAD; writes evidence under .ddx/executions/ac-<timestamp>/; prints pass/block/error per check; exit 1 on any block/error
- cli/cmd/ac_test.go — invokes via cobra Execute against a tmp repo with a fixture check; asserts exit code and printed output

OUT OF SCOPE (separate child bead)
- pre_merge hook in execute_bead_loop.go
- checks_bypass annotation parsing + event recording
- e2e tests via ddx try

ACCEPTANCE
1. cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go exist with documented types matching parent design.
2. Loader reads .ddx/checks/*.yaml; unknown fields and bad YAML produce errors with file:line.
3. Runner injects env vars, executes checks in parallel, reads result JSON, treats non-zero exit as status=error, missing result file as status=error.
4. \`ddx ac run <bead-id>\` subcommand exists, runs configured checks, prints summary, exits 1 on block/error.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... -run 'Ac|Checks' green.
6. lefthook pre-commit passes for changed files." 2>&1 | tail -3
input.description: Create child bead 1: protocol package + ac CLI

data.output:
Create a new bead

Usage:
  ddx bead create <title> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    Acceptance criteria
      --description string   Description
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --labels string        Comma-separated labels
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority (0=highest, 4=lowest) (default 2)
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --type string          Bead type (task, bug, epic, chore)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

input.command:
ddx bead create "checks protocol package: types + loader + runner + ddx ac CLI" \
  --priority 0 --labels "phase:2,area:checks,kind:platform" \
  --parent ddx-a946c744 \
  --description "Slice 1 of parent ddx-a946c744. Build the language-agnostic check protocol package and the standalone developer CLI. NO integration with execute-bead loop yet (that is child slice 3).

CONTEXT
See parent ddx-a946c744 for full design rationale. This slice creates the foundational package and developer-facing CLI for running checks against the current worktree.

SCOPE
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — Check, AppliesTo, Result, Violation types; status enum (pass/block/error); JSON schema doc as Go comments
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml; reject invalid YAML and unknown fields with file:line; return []Check
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — given []Check + invocation context (BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID), run all applicable checks in parallel via os/exec; read EVIDENCE_DIR/<name>.json; non-zero exit -> status=error; return []Result
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for path glob and label matching (AppliesTo); loader valid/invalid yaml; runner with shell-script fixture under testdata/ verifying env injection, parallel exec, exit-code handling, missing result file -> error
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] subcommand wired to runner; reads bead, computes DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD from bead metadata + current HEAD; writes evidence under .ddx/executions/ac-<ts>/; prints results; exits 1 on any block/error
- cli/cmd/ac_test.go — invokes via cobra Execute against tmp repo with fixture check; asserts exit code and output

OUT OF SCOPE
- pre_merge hook in execute_bead_loop.go (slice 3)
- checks_bypass annotation parsing + event recording (slice 3)
- e2e tests via ddx try (slice 3)" \
  --acceptance "1. cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go exist with documented types matching parent design.
2. Loader reads .ddx/checks/*.yaml; unknown fields and bad YAML produce errors with file:line.
3. Runner injects documented env vars, executes checks in parallel, reads result JSON, treats non-zero exit and missing result file as status=error.
4. ddx ac run <bead-id> subcommand exists, runs configured checks, prints summary, exits 1 on block/error.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... -run 'Ac|Checks' green.
6. lefthook pre-commit passes for changed files." 2>&1 | tail -5

ddx bead create "checks integration: pre_merge hook in execute-bead loop + checks_bypass annotation + e2e" \
  --priority 0 --labels "phase:2,area:agent,area:checks,kind:platform,prevention" \
  --parent ddx-a946c744 \
  --description "Slice 2 of parent ddx-a946c744. Wire the checks runner (built in child slice ddx-aee651ec) into the execute-bead loop as a pre_merge gate, add the checks_bypass bead annotation with event recording, and add e2e tests.

DEPENDS ON
- ddx-aee651ec (checks protocol package + ddx ac CLI)

CONTEXT
See parent ddx-a946c744 for full design. This slice integrates the protocol package into the merge flow and adds the bypass mechanism.

SCOPE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — call cli/internal/checks runner for all applicable checks BEFORE the merge-back step; on any status=block/error, abort merge, preserve worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions, write evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/, record event
- cli/internal/bead schema — checks_bypass annotation: list of {name, reason, bead}; missing reason -> bypass rejected loudly; on bypass, named check is skipped and a bypass event is recorded with the reason
- E2E test 1: fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with script that always returns status=block; ddx try on a no-op bead aborts merge, preserves worktree, evidence file present, event recorded
- E2E test 2: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on bead -> merge proceeds, bypass event recorded
- Tests under cli/internal/agent/ and/or cli/cmd/ following existing execute_bead_*_test.go patterns

OUT OF SCOPE
- New checks for any specific language (separate REACH-* beads)" \
  --acceptance "1. execute_bead_loop pre_merge step runs all applicable checks (filtered by AppliesTo) in parallel before merge-back.
2. Any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
3. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason -> bypass rejected.
4. E2E test: dummy-fail fixture causes merge abort, worktree preserved, evidence written, event recorded.
5. E2E test: dummy-fail + checks_bypass -> merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
6. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows fixture wrote result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.
7. cd cli && go test ./... green; lefthook pre-commit passes." 2>&1 | tail -3

ddx bead dep add ddx-3e60fd84 ddx-aee651ec 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
ddx bead update ddx-a946c744 --notes "decomposed into ddx-aee651ec (protocol package + ddx ac CLI) and ddx-3e60fd84 (pre_merge integration + checks_bypass + e2e). ddx-3e60fd84 depends on ddx-aee651ec." 2>&1 | tail -3
ddx bead dep tree ddx-a946c744 2>&1 | head -20

This slice integrates the protocol package into the merge flow and adds the bypass mechanism.\n\nSCOPE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — call cli/internal/checks runner for all applicable checks BEFORE the merge-back step; on any status=block/error, abort merge, preserve worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions, write evidence under .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/, record event\n- cli/internal/bead schema — checks_bypass annotation: list of {name, reason, bead}; missing reason -\u003e bypass rejected loudly; on bypass, named check is skipped and a bypass event is recorded with the reason\n- E2E test 1: fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with script that always returns status=block; ddx try on a no-op bead aborts merge, preserves worktree, evidence file present, event recorded\n- E2E test 2: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on bead -\u003e merge proceeds, bypass event recorded\n- Tests under cli/internal/agent/ and/or cli/cmd/ following existing execute_bead_*_test.go patterns\n\nOUT OF SCOPE\n- New checks for any specific language (separate REACH-* beads)",

- ddx-3e60fd84 — pre_merge hook integration in execute_bead_loop.go + checks_bypass bead annotation + event recording + two e2e fixture tests. Depends on ddx-aee651ec.

data.text:
Decomposed ddx-a946c744 into two children rather than implementing in one attempt:

- **ddx-aee651ec** — checks protocol package (`cli/internal/checks/{protocol,loader,runner}.go` + tests) and `ddx ac run` CLI. Self-contained, no agent loop touch.
- **ddx-3e60fd84** — `pre_merge` hook in `execute_bead_loop.go`, `checks_bypass` bead annotation with event recording, and two e2e fixture tests. Depends on ddx-aee651ec.

Dep edge recorded; parent updated with decomposition notes; no_changes_rationale.txt written under the per-attempt evidence dir. Reasoning: 10 ACs spanning a new package + 1693-line loop integration + new bead schema field + new CLI command + two e2e tests is well past the size threshold and benefits from independent review.
